methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing topographic data of Glaciar Jorge Montt using various remote sensing techniques over different years.

steps.description: The first detailed topography of the area was based upon the McHurd aerial photographs of 1975, prepared by the Military Geographical Institute of Chile (IGM) at 1:50 000 scale.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain topographic data from McHurd aerial photographs of 1975

steps.description: The map includes the ice front position of Glaciar Jorge Montt and the surface topography of the glacier up to approx. 1000 m a.s.l., where the stereoscopic capabilities of the aerial photography was lost due to white surfaces of fresh snow.
steps.searchable_summary: process map to include ice front position and surface topography

steps.description: In 2000, the Patagonian icefields region was included in the SRTM DEM dataset with resolution of 90 m for Patagonia.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch SRTM DEM dataset for Patagonian icefields

steps.description: Hypsometric curves of the main glaciers were derived from SRTM data using the glacier basins for the same year (2000).
steps.searchable_summary: derive hypsometric curves from SRTM data

steps.description: In December 2002 and 2008, airborne campaigns were conducted from a Chilean Navy Orion P3 airplane using the NASA Airborne Topographic Mapper (ATM) laser to survey the surface topography of Glaciar Jorge Montt.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct airborne campaigns using NASA ATM laser
outputs: surface topography data with decimeter accuracy

steps.description: The ASTER scene acquired in 16 February 2010 was selected for an ASTER DMO (on demand) DEM, with spatial resolution of 30 m and vertical accuracy of 21 m.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire ASTER DMO DEM for February 2010
outputs: ASTER DMO DEM with 30 m resolution
